hidden_axioms.name: Objective Truth
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A1 (free will via performative contradiction) requires that there is an objective truth that is falsified by the denial. Without objective truth, the contradiction is not a contradiction.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: HA-001 (same requirement, explicitly identified in Phase 0.9)

hidden_axioms.name: Reliable Reason
hidden_axioms.description: The performative contradiction argument requires that reason is a reliable guide to truth. If reason is unreliable, the self-refutation argument fails.

hidden_axioms.name: Non-Arbitrariness of Logical Inference
hidden_axioms.description: Any derivation in CE-003 requires that inference rules are non-arbitrary. Modus ponens must be valid. This is not stated.

hidden_axioms.name: Identity Persistence
hidden_axioms.description: Self-ownership (MS-A2) requires that the same person who mixes labour at time T is the owner at time T+1. This requires a theory of personal identity across time. CE-003 does not state it.
hidden_axioms.extra_note: Especially important for property rights: does inherited property require a theory of identity-through-death?

hidden_axioms.name: Moral Realism
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A3 (NAP) says coercion is 'categorically impermissible.' The word 'categorically' imports moral realism: there are objective moral facts, not merely preferences or conventions. CE-003 does not state this.
hidden_axioms.extra_note: Without moral realism, NAP is merely a PREFERENCE ('I prefer not to be coerced') not a CATEGORICAL PROHIBITION. The entire anti-statism derivation depends on NAP's categorical character.

hidden_axioms.name: Definition of Coercion
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A3 prohibits 'initiating coercion.' This requires a definition of coercion that CE-003 does not provide. Is withholding a resource coercion? Is environmental pollution coercion? Is monopoly coercion? Each answer requires additional axioms.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: Rule of Taslīṭ (A-000008) provides explicit definition of property sovereignty
hidden_axioms.extra_note: The Rothbardian tradition has produced hundreds of pages on what counts as aggression. CE-003 treats this as settled when it is not.

hidden_axioms.name: Original Acquisition Justice
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A2 (homesteading) requires that first-possession of unowned resources is just. This is the Lockean proviso problem: what if all resources are already owned? Or what if a first-possessor claims monopoly on a scarce resource? CE-003 does not resolve this.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: Rule of Taslīṭ + Dignitary Equality (A-000009) together address this
hidden_axioms.extra_note: Nozick's Anarchy, State, and Utopia devotes 20 pages to this and still does not fully resolve it.

hidden_axioms.name: Rational Expected-Utility Maximization
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A4 (unknown-horizon posit) only solves the last-round problem if agents are rational expected-utility maximizers who update their strategies on terminal-period probability. CE-003 does not state this assumption.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: None — Resurrection operates independently of rationality assumption
hidden_axioms.extra_note: Critical: if agents are NOT EU-maximizers (e.g., they are time-inconsistent, hyperbolic discounters, or simply vengeful), MS-A4 does not stabilize cooperation.

hidden_axioms.name: Common Knowledge of MS-A4
hidden_axioms.description: For MS-A4 to stabilize cooperation game-theoretically, it must be COMMON KNOWLEDGE among all players that the terminal period is unknowable. If even one player believes otherwise, the cooperative equilibrium breaks down from their end. CE-003 does not state this.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: Resurrection is a shared cultural-metaphysical belief providing natural common knowledge
hidden_axioms.extra_note: This is a crucial disanalogy with Resurrection. Resurrection provides common knowledge through shared cultural-religious practice. MS-A4 is a philosophical posit that rational agents must individually accept.

hidden_axioms.name: Counterfactual Reasoning for Aggression Identification
hidden_axioms.description: To apply NAP, agents must determine who 'initiated' coercion. This requires counterfactual reasoning: 'what would have happened without this action?' CE-003 provides no specification for how counterfactuals are evaluated.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: Implied through the property-sovereignty framework but similarly not fully specified

hidden_axioms.name: Natural Person Concept
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A2 (self-ownership) applies to 'persons.' What is a person? Does it include corporations, animals, future generations, AI systems? CE-003 does not define 'person.'
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: A-000009 (dignitary equality) + A-000001 (free will) together define personhood

hidden_axioms.name: Causal Regularity
hidden_axioms.description: For CE-003's derivations to function, causes must reliably produce effects — so that coercion can be identified as causing harm, property can be traced to labour, etc. CE-003 assumes causal regularity without stating it.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: HA-003 (same requirement in Theory of Liberty)

hidden_axioms.name: Temporal Epistemic Stability of MS-A4
hidden_axioms.description: MS-A4 says the terminal period is 'unknowable at any time.' But what happens when an agent observes a near-terminal signal (e.g., they are dying, or the counterparty is dying)? Does MS-A4 hold even when strong probabilistic evidence suggests T is near? If MS-A4 allows for near-terminal beliefs, backward induction can resume from that near-terminal belief. CE-003 does not address this.
hidden_axioms.parallel_in_ToL: Resurrection: the afterlife makes ALL terminals unknowable because the game continues beyond physical death — not merely 'we don't know when T is' but 'T ≥ ∞ is structurally guaranteed'

effective_axiom_burden: CE-003 has 4 named + 13 hidden = 17 total axioms. The Theory of Liberty has 6 named + 12 hidden = 18 total axioms. CE-003's apparent parsimony (4 vs 6 named) disappears when hidden axioms are counted (17 vs 18). CE-003 has nearly identical axiom burden to the Theory of Liberty.

comparison_with_ToL.parsimony_advantage: NONE — CE-003 saves 2 named axioms but adds 1 hidden axiom. Effectively equivalent.
